name: Loanstar Title Loans
description_short: LoanStar Title Loans provides vehicle title-based loans up to $10,000 with same-day funding at multiple Texas locations. Borrowers pledge their car title as collateral to access emergency cash.
description_long:
LoanStar Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over 25 years as a vehicle equity lending company. The Fort Worth location at 8304 Camp Bowie is one of six regional branches serving the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex and surrounding areas. The company positions itself as helping working Americans access cash by leveraging the equity in their vehicles.

LoanStar offers auto title loans (also called car title loans) ranging up to $10,000. The application requires only three items: the vehicle itself, a valid photo ID, and a clear vehicle title. The company accepts multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union transfers. Loans are available through both multiple installment plans and single installment structures, with published fee schedules for disclosure at $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts. The Fort Worth location operates Monday-Friday 10 AM–6 PM and Saturday 9 AM–2 PM.

LoanStar differentiates itself through its established 25-year track record and multi-location presence in Texas. The company provides transparent fee schedules and disclosure documents upfront, and advertises fast access to cash. The brand emphasizes accessibility by operating at convenient hours including Saturday mornings and maintaining multiple neighborhood locations rather than a single centralized facility.

Title loans carry inherent risks that borrowers should carefully consider. The primary caveat is that failure to repay results in vehicle repossession, as the lender holds the title as collateral. While the company discloses fee schedules, the actual APR and total cost of borrowing depend on individual loan terms and should be compared against alternatives like payday loans, personal loans, or credit union PALs before proceeding.
address: 1340 E Belt Line Rd, Richardson, TX 75081

company_info.headquarters: 1340 E Belt Line Rd, Richardson, TX 75081

pros: Fast funding with same-day or next-day cash available for emergency needs
Loans up to $10,000—higher than typical payday loans ($100-$1,000)
Simple application: only vehicle, photo ID, and clear title required
Multiple convenient locations across Dallas-Fort Worth (6 branches documented)
Extended Saturday hours (9 AM–2 PM) for working customers
Multiple payment methods accepted (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Published fee schedules and disclosure documents provided for transparency
cons: Vehicle repossession risk if loan is not repaid—collateral-based debt obligation
Title loans typically carry high APRs; actual rates not transparently listed on website
Limited online information about APR, fees, and total cost of borrowing before contact
Requires clear vehicle title—excludes borrowers with liens or loans against their car
No mention of hardship programs, extensions, or flexible repayment options on website
best_for: Vehicle owners facing emergency cash needs who can repay within a short timeframe
Borrowers with poor credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
Individuals needing amounts larger than payday loans ($100–$1,000) but under $10,000
Dallas-Fort Worth residents with convenient access to multiple branch locations
diagnosis: LoanStar Title Loans is best for vehicle owners in the Dallas-Fort Worth area facing genuine emergencies who need $1,000–$10,000 and can repay within weeks or months. The critical caveat is that title loans risk vehicle repossession if repayment fails, making them a last-resort option that should only be considered after exploring payday alternatives (credit union PALs under 36% APR) and personal loans from traditional lenders.
services: Auto title loans (vehicle equity loans) up to $10,000
Multiple installment loan plans with published fee schedules
Same-day or next-day funding for qualified borrowers
In-store cash payments at multiple Fort Worth and surrounding area locations
Fee schedule disclosures for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts
OCCC (Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner) compliance documentation
Spanish language support (ES option on website)
Directions and store locator for six regional locations
